2606.27106.title: Application of LLMs to Threat Assessment of Foreign Peacekeeping Missions
2606.27106.paper_summary: We present a novel approach for applying Large Language Models (LLMs) to threat assessment in the context of foreign peacekeeping missions. Building on the PINPOINT project and its use case, the EU Monitoring Mission in Georgia, we combine an interdisciplinary risk-model with OSINT-based media collection and LLM-supported threat extraction. The proposed workflow maps media contents to mission-relevant threats, extracts structured information and applies several additional LLM-based processing steps to improve relevance and grounding. An evaluation of threats extracted from media documents shows high agreement between automatically generated results and human judgment for core aspects such as threat and mission relevance. These results indicate that LLMs provide a promising approach to support analysts in the context of peacekeeping missions.
2606.27106.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一種新穎的方法，將大型語言模型（LLMs）應用於外國維和任務的威脅評估。基於PINPOINT項目及其用例——歐盟在喬治亞的監察任務，我們結合了跨學科的風險模型、基於開源情報的媒體收集和LLM支持的威脅提取。所提出的工作流程將媒體內容映射到與任務相關的威脅，提取結構化信息，並應用幾個額外的基於LLM的處理步驟，以提高相關性和基礎性。對從媒體文件中提取的威脅的評估顯示，自動生成的結果與人類判斷在威脅和任務相關性等核心方面之間具有高度一致性。這些結果表明，LLMs為支持分析師在維和任務中的工作提供了一種有前景的方法。
2606.27106.author: Gerhard Backfried et.al.
2606.27106.authors: Gerhard Backfried, Christian Schmidt, Diego Pilutti, Michael Suker
